definition: Strong aggressive price move (1-3 large-bodied candles) proving institutional commitment.
depends_on: liquidity_sweep (displacement follows confirmed sweep — it's the distribution start)
feeds_into: order_blocks (the last opposing candle before displacement IS the OB)
optimal_trade_entry (Fibonacci applied to the displacement move defines OTE)
related_to: power_of_three_and_AMD (displacement IS the start of distribution phase)
characteristics: Large candle body (70%+ body, minimal wicks)
Occurs at start of distribution phase
critical_chain: Without displacement, there is no FVG, no OB, and no OTE. Displacement is the PROOF of institutional entry. Everything in layers 4-5 depends on displacement having occurred.
what_it_proves: That the liquidity_sweep was manipulation (not breakout). That institutions have committed to the new direction. That the OB and FVG created are valid entry zones.

trading_rules.rule_1: Displacement MUST create at least one FVG — if no FVG formed, the move is not true displacement
trading_rules.rule_2: Displacement candles should have 70%+ body (minimal wicks) — large wicks suggest indecision, not commitment
trading_rules.rule_3: Displacement after a confirmed sweep = highest-confidence signal. Displacement without prior sweep = lower confidence (could be news spike).
trading_rules.rule_4: The last opposing candle before displacement = the Order Block entry zone. This is your pullback entry level.
trading_rules.rule_5: If displacement breaks structure (BOS/CHoCH), it confirms the new direction. Without structure break, it may be a false move within the range.

bridge_integration.detection: Tracked via displacement_confirmed flag. Requires sweep + FVG in opposite direction = proven manipulation.
bridge_integration.prompt_display: Shows as 'CONFIRMED (sweep + FVG reversal)' or 'NOT CONFIRMED' in Claude prompt.
bridge_integration.score_impact: Displacement confirmed = highest-confidence setup. Without it, OB and FVG entries are lower probability.
